trials.justification: All pass criteria met. Deprioritisation consistently framed as a reasonable trade-off ('the known risk appeared low given the provider's assurance', 'reasonable at the time'). Five contributing factors identified (normalisation gap, upstream change without notice, exact-string matching, silent-fail reconciliation, no assumption-staleness tracking). Equivalent of 'given what we knew' used throughout ('given the information available', 'Why it made sense' column, 'reasonable deprioritisation during development'). No blame language toward Bird, Shaq, or Coach K — Shaq's implementation explicitly called 'the correct call given the information available', Bird's flag described neutrally. Forward Commitments include two PROCESS actions: go-live risk reassessment gate (item 6) and change-notification SLA (item 7). What We Learned uses durable 'We now know that...' framing throughout, with no blanket 'always validate' rules.

observations.text: Timeline's 'Why it made sense' column is an excellent structural choice — it forces contextual framing for every decision, making blame nearly impossible
Contributing factors section explicitly states 'Removing any single one would likely have prevented or significantly reduced the impact' — distributes causality correctly
Shaq's implementation decision explicitly defended: 'Implementing it unilaterally would have contradicted the prioritisation decision and delayed delivery'
Factor 5 nails the assumption-staleness insight: 'The tech debt item was treated as static rather than as a risk that compounds once the system is in production'
Silent-fail reconciliation job surfaced as its own factor with the key insight that 'a single unmatched financial record is significant regardless of volume'
Learnings avoid the 'always implement data validation' trap — instead frames it as 'provider assurances are point-in-time statements, not contracts'
Forward Commitments go beyond 'add normalisation' to include process-level changes (go-live reassessment gate, provider change-notification SLA)
All 6 sections present and substantive, with Preserving the Learning section adding ADR, domain rule, runbook, and checklist items

## 1. Situation Snapshot

The Invoicing Service ingests supplier invoice data via an external webhook. Starting 2026-02-28, a subset of invoice records were persisted with float-formatted `supplier_id` values (e.g., `"12345.0"` instead of `"12345"`), caused by a new sub-provider onboarded by the webhook partner. The records were syntactically val

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: deprioritisation consistently framed as a reasonable trade-off ('normal and defensible trade-off', 'real constraint', 'reduced perceived likelihood'); 5 contributing factors identified (no normalisation in context, unannounced sub-provider onboarding, silent-fail reconciliation, no assumption-staleness tracking, no schema validation) exceeding the 4-factor minimum; equivalent of 'given what we knew' framing present throughout the timeline's 'What was known' / 'Why it made sense' columns; zero instances of negligence language or Bird's flag described as 'ignored' — it is consistently described as 'identified', 'logged', 'flagged'; Forward Commitments include a [PROCESS] action for tech-debt review cadence plus a sprint planning checklist for recording deprioritisation assumptions; What We Learned uses 'We now know that...' systemic framing throughout with no blame assignment.

observations.text: Timeline's 4-column structure (When / What was known / Decision made / Why it made sense) is an exceptionally effective device for preventing hindsight bias — every decision is anchored to the information available at the time
Contributing Factors opens with 'Each of the following factors was independently necessary' — correctly frames causation as multi-factor, not single-root-cause
Shaq's role explicitly defended: 'Adding out-of-scope work unilaterally would have been a different kind of process failure' — no blame for implementing as directed
The learning about deprioritised risks ('the risk acceptance became permanent by default') is framed as a system property, not a personal failure of Coach K
Forward Commitments use clear PREVENT/MITIGATE/DETECT/PROCESS tags with owners and targets — actionable and categorised
Section 6 (Preserving the Learning) goes beyond the minimum by embedding learnings into ADRs, domain rules, and checklists — durable institutional memory

Minor: the learning 'defensive normalisation at ingestion boundaries should not be gated on whether the current provider usually sends the right format' edges toward a blanket rule, though it is saved by being scoped to 'ingestion boundaries' and grounded in the specific insight about point-in-time confirmations

## 1. Situation Snapshot

On 2026-02-28, the Invoicing Service began persisting invoice records with malformed `supplier_id` values after an external webhook started sending float-formatted IDs (e.g., `"12345.0"` instead of `"12345"`). The malformed records were valid at the persistence layer but caused silent failures in a downstream reconciliation job that relies on exact-string matching. The mismatch went undetected for
